Only include tools you want to override — omitted tools keep defaults.

delete.description:
Disconnect the project's GitHub App.

Wipes stored credentials, removes every installation row, invalidates the
cached client, and drops any pending manifest-state entry. The actual
GitHub App on github.com is *not* deleted — GitHub does not expose an API
for that. The UI surfaces a deep-link to ``/apps/{slug}/advanced`` so the
user can finish the cleanup manually.

get.description: Handle GitHub App installation callback redirect.
List the project's GitHub App installations.

schema.title: Response List Installations Api Projects  Slug  Github Installations Get

post.description: Manually add a GitHub App installation.
Re-fetch the repo list for an installation from the GitHub API.

schema.title: Response Sync Installation Api Projects  Slug  Github Installations  Installation Id  Sync Post

get.description:
Handle GitHub's redirect after the user creates a GitHub App from our manifest.

Exchanges the temporary ``code`` for the App's credentials and stores
them in ``project_settings``.

post.description:
Prepare a GitHub App manifest for the UI to POST to github.com.

The UI must submit a real top-level HTML form POST — GitHub only
accepts this flow as a browser navigation, not an XHR.
